- [Address](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/address) — A verified destination email address on the account.
- [AddressMap](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/addressing/addressmap) — A Cloudflare Address Map — assigns account-owned or Cloudflare-assigned static IPs to zones (BYOIP / Enterprise static IPs).
- [AllowPolicy](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/allowpolicy) — A Cloudflare Email Security (Area 1) allow policy — exempts messages matching a sender/recipient pattern from detections.
- [App](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/calls/app) — A Cloudflare Realtime (formerly \"Calls\") SFU application.
- [App](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/flagship/app) — A Cloudflare Flagship app — a container for feature flags.
- [App](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/realtimekit/app) — A Cloudflare RealtimeKit app — the organizational container for RealtimeKit meetings, presets, and webhooks.
- [Application](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/application) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Access application.
- [Application](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/spectrum/application) — A Cloudflare Spectrum application — DDoS protection and proxying for arbitrary TCP/UDP services (SSH, Minecraft, RDP, custom protocols), not just HTTP.
- [BgpPrefix](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/addressing/bgpprefix) — A BGP prefix controlling on-demand advertisement of a BYOIP prefix (or a subnet of it) to the internet.
- [BlockSender](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/blocksender) — A Cloudflare Email Security (Area 1) blocked sender — messages matching the pattern are blocked before delivery.
- [Bookmark](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/bookmark) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Access bookmark application — an unprotected link shown in the App Launcher.
- [BotManagement](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/botmanagement/botmanagement) — The bot-management configuration of a Cloudflare zone — a zone-scoped **singleton**: every zone always has exactly one bot-management config, so there is no create or delete on the Cloudflare side. Reconciling this resource adopts the singleton and PUTs only the fields you explicitly set, leaving every other field untouched.
- [Browser](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/browser) — A Cloudflare Browser Rendering binding for launching headless browser sessions from Workers — a Worker-only binding with no backing cloud resource.
- [Bucket](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/r2/bucket) — A Cloudflare R2 object storage bucket with S3-compatible API.
- [BucketEventNotification](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/r2/bucketeventnotification) — Event notifications for a Cloudflare R2 bucket, delivered to a Queue.
- [BucketSippy](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/r2/bucketsippy) — Sippy — incremental migration from AWS S3 or Google Cloud Storage into a Cloudflare R2 bucket.
- [cache](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/cache) — Enable [Workers Cache](https://blog.cloudflare.com/workers-cache/) on the surrounding Worker and get the runtime cache client.
- [CatalogSync](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magiccloudnetworking/catalogsync) — A Magic Cloud Networking catalog sync — continuously materializes the catalog of discovered cloud resources (filtered by a policy expression) into a destination such as a Zero Trust list.
- [CatchAll](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/catchall) — The Cloudflare Email Routing catch-all rule for a zone.
- [Certificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/certificate) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Access mTLS certificate. Uploads a CA certificate that Access uses to validate client certificates presented to protected applications on the associated hostnames.
- [Certificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/gateway/certificate) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Gateway certificate — a Cloudflare-generated CA used by Gateway to inspect TLS traffic (HTTPS filtering, antivirus scanning, browser isolation). The certificate body is generated by Cloudflare; you only choose the validity period and whether it is activated (deployed to the edge).
- [Certificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/origintlsclientauth/certificate) — A zone-level Authenticated Origin Pulls (AOP) client certificate (`/zones/{zone_id}/origin_tls_client_auth`).
- [CertificatePack](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ssl/certificatepack) — An Advanced Certificate Manager (ACM) certificate pack — an order for edge certificates covering a custom set of hostnames in a zone, with a choice of Certificate Authority, validation method, and validity period.
- [ClientCertificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/clientcertificate/clientcertificate) — A zone-level API Shield mTLS client certificate signed by the Cloudflare Managed CA.
- [CloudIntegration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magiccloudnetworking/cloudintegration) — A Magic Cloud Networking cloud integration — registers an AWS, Azure, or GCP account with Cloudflare so Magic Cloud Networking can discover its networking resources (VPCs, subnets, gateways, …).
- [CmbConfig](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/logscontrol/cmbconfig) — The account-level Customer Metadata Boundary (CMB) configuration for Cloudflare Logs (`/accounts/{account_id}/logs/control/cmb/config`).
- [Config](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magicnetworkmonitoring/config) — The Magic Network Monitoring (MNM) account configuration — the singleton that registers your network's routers (and optionally WARP devices) as flow-data sources and sets the fallback packet sampling rate.
- [Config](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/zaraz/config) — A Cloudflare Zaraz zone configuration.
- [Configuration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/apishield/configuration) — The API Shield configuration of a Cloudflare zone — the session identifiers (\"auth ID characteristics\") used to attribute API traffic to individual consumers for API Discovery and volumetric abuse detection.
- [Configuration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/gateway/configuration) — Manages the **singleton** Cloudflare Zero Trust **Gateway configuration** for an account (`/accounts/{accountId}/gateway/configuration`) — account-wide settings like activity logging, TLS decryption, the block page, anti-virus scanning, and browser isolation.
- [Configuration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/configuration) — Routing configuration for a remotely-managed Cloudflare Tunnel.
- [Connect](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/hyperdrive/connect) — A typed accessor for a Cloudflare Hyperdrive runtime binding inside a Worker. Provides the same shape as the raw `Hyperdrive` runtime object (connection string, host, port, user, password, database) plus a `raw` escape hatch for libraries that want direct access.
- [Connection](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/hyperdrive/connection) — A Cloudflare Hyperdrive configuration.
- [Consumer](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/queues/consumer) — A Cloudflare Queue Consumer that processes messages from a Queue.
- [Container](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/containers/container) — A Cloudflare Container that runs a long-lived process alongside a Durable Object.
- [ContainerApplication](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/containers/containerapplication) — A Cloudflare Container Application — the deployed, scalable unit that runs a containerized program on Cloudflare's compute platform. Alchemy bundles the `main` entrypoint, builds a Docker image, pushes it to the Cloudflare registry, and reconciles the application's scaling and runtime configuration.
- [ContentScanning](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/contentscanning/contentscanning) — WAF Content Scanning (malicious uploads detection) on a Cloudflare zone — the `/zones/{zone_id}/content-upload-scan/settings` singleton toggle.
- [cron](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/cron) — Subscribe to Cloudflare Cron Triggers with an Effect handler.
- [CustomCertificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/customcertificate/customcertificate) — A Cloudflare custom (BYO) edge certificate — upload your own SSL certificate and private key to be served at Cloudflare's edge for a zone.
- [CustomHostname](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/customhostname/customhostname) — A Cloudflare for SaaS custom hostname.
- [CustomNameserver](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/customnameserver/customnameserver) — An account-level custom (vanity) nameserver — e.g. `ns1.yourbrand.com` — that zones on the account can use instead of their assigned `*.ns.cloudflare.com` names.
- [CustomNameservers](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/zone/customnameservers) — Controls whether a Cloudflare zone uses **account-level custom nameservers** (ACNS, `/zones/{zone_id}/custom_ns`).
- [CustomPage](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/custompage) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Access custom page. Replaces the default Access block pages (`identity_denied` / `forbidden`) with custom HTML, which can then be selected on an Access application.
- [CustomRuleset](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ruleset/customruleset) — A standalone account-level Cloudflare ruleset (`kind: \"custom\"`).
- [CustomTopics](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/customtopics) — Custom topic categories for AI Security for Apps (Firewall for AI) content detection on a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/ai-security/custom-topics`).
- [CustomTrustStore](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/acm/customtruststore) — A root CA certificate in a zone's custom origin trust store (`/zones/{zone_id}/acm/custom_trust_store`). Cloudflare uses the trust store to validate your origin server's certificate when connecting to the origin (e.g. with Full (strict) SSL and a private CA at the origin).
- [Database](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/d1/database) — A Cloudflare D1 serverless SQL database built on SQLite.
- [DataCatalog](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/r2/datacatalog) — Apache Iceberg data catalog attached to a Cloudflare R2 bucket.
- [Dataset](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/dataset) — A saved log filter (\"dataset\") on a Cloudflare.AI. Gateway.
- [DdosAllowlistEntry](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ddosprotection/ddosallowlistentry) — An Advanced TCP Protection allowlist entry (Magic Transit).
- [Deployment](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pages/deployment) — A direct-upload deployment on a Cloudflare Pages project.
- [DetectionSettings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/fraud/detectionsettings) — The fraud-detection (Fraud User Profiles) settings of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/fraud_detection/settings`) — a zone-scoped **singleton**: every zone always has exactly one fraud-detection settings object, so there is no create or delete on the Cloudflare side. Reconciling this resource adopts the singleton and PUTs only the fields you explicitly set, leaving every other field untouched.
- [DeviceCustomProfile](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/devices/devicecustomprofile) — A Cloudflare WARP **custom device profile** — a settings profile applied to the subset of devices matched by a wirefilter `match` expression at a given `precedence`.
- [DeviceDefaultProfile](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/devices/devicedefaultprofile) — Manages the **singleton** Cloudflare WARP **default device profile** for an account. The default profile applies to every WARP device not matched by a custom profile.
- [DeviceDexTest](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/devices/devicedextest) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **DEX synthetic test** — an HTTP or traceroute probe that enrolled WARP devices run on a schedule so the Digital Experience Monitoring dashboard can chart reachability and latency to your critical applications.
- [DeviceManagedNetwork](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/devices/devicemanagednetwork) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **device managed network** — a TLS endpoint the WARP client probes to detect whether the device is on a known network. Device profiles can then `match` on `network` to apply different WARP settings on trusted networks.
- [DevicePostureIntegration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/devices/devicepostureintegration) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **device posture integration** — a service-to- service connection to a third-party endpoint security provider (CrowdStrike, Intune, Kolide, Workspace ONE, ...) whose signals power `*_s2s` device posture rules.
- [DevicePostureRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/devices/deviceposturerule) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **device posture rule** — a periodic check the WARP client runs on enrolled devices (OS version, firewall status, disk encryption, file presence, or a third-party security provider's verdict). Posture results can then gate Access policies and Gateway rules.
- [DeviceSettings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/devices/devicesettings) — Manages the **singleton** Cloudflare Zero Trust **device settings** for an account (`/accounts/{accountId}/devices/settings`) — account-wide WARP toggles like the Gateway TCP/UDP proxy, managed root certificate installation, and CGNAT virtual IP.
- [DirectoryService](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/connectivity/directoryservice) — A Cloudflare Connectivity Directory service — a named entry in the account's private-network service directory that maps a service name to a private host (IP or hostname) reachable through a Cloudflare Tunnel.
- [DispatchNamespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workersforplatforms/dispatchnamespace) — A Workers for Platforms dispatch namespace — a container for customer (\"user\") Workers that a platform Worker dispatches to at runtime via a dynamic-dispatch binding.
- [Dnssec](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/dnssec) — DNSSEC configuration for a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/dnssec`).
- [Domain](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/domain) — A Cloudflare Email Security (Area 1) domain's settings.
- [Domain](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pages/domain) — A custom domain attached to a Cloudflare Pages project.
- [Domain](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/registrar/domain) — The mutable Cloudflare Registrar settings (`auto_renew`, `locked`, `privacy`) on a domain that is already registered with Cloudflare Registrar.
- [DurableObject](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/durableobject) — A Cloudflare Durable Object namespace that manages globally unique, stateful instances with WebSocket hibernation support.
- [DurableObjectChatPersistence](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/durableobjectchatpersistence) — A `BackingPersistence` layer (Effect AI persistence module) backed by the surrounding Durable Object's `state.storage`. Drop-in storage for `Persistence.layerResultPersisted({ storeId })` so chat history, cached AI responses, or any other persisted state lives in the DO SQLite store with `${storeId}:` key namespacing.
- [EndpointHealthcheck](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/diagnostics/endpointhealthcheck) — A Magic Transit / Magic WAN endpoint healthcheck — a continuous ICMP probe of an on-net IP address used to monitor reachability of hosts behind Magic tunnels.
- [Entry](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dlp/entry) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **DLP custom entry** — a standalone regular- expression detection that can be attached to a custom DLP profile. Use it when entries are managed independently of the Profile that groups them.
- [Evaluation](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/evaluation) — An evaluation job on a Cloudflare.AI. Gateway.
- [Expression](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/contentscanning/expression) — A custom scan expression (\"payload\") for WAF Content Scanning — tells the malicious-uploads scanner where to find encoded or nested content in the request body (`/zones/{zone_id}/content-upload-scan/payloads`).
- [FallbackOrigin](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/customhostname/fallbackorigin) — The Cloudflare for SaaS fallback origin of a zone.
- [Firewall](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/firewall) — A Cloudflare DNS Firewall cluster.
- [Flag](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/flagship/flag) — A feature flag in a Cloudflare Flagship app.
- [Gateway](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/gateway) — A Cloudflare.AI. Gateway for observability, caching, rate limiting, and governance across AI provider requests.
- [GatewayDynamicRouting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/gatewaydynamicrouting) — A dynamic routing configuration (\"route\") on a Cloudflare.AI. Gateway.
- [GatewayProvider](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/gatewayprovider) — A BYOK (bring-your-own-key) provider credential on a Cloudflare AI Gateway.
- [Get](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workersforplatforms/get) — Bind a DispatchNamespace to a Worker and obtain the Effect-native dynamic-dispatch client (`get`).
- [GitHubRepositoryEventSourceLive](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/githubrepositoryeventsourcelive) — GitHub event source for Cloudflare Workers.
- [GoogleTagGateway](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/googletaggateway/googletaggateway) — Google Tag Gateway configuration for a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/settings/google-tag-gateway/config`).
- [GreTunnel](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/gretunnel) — A Magic Transit / Magic WAN GRE tunnel between Cloudflare and a customer router.
- [Group](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/group) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Access group — a reusable, account-scoped set of Access rule criteria. Groups are referenced from Access policies via a `{ group: { id } }` rule, letting many policies share one membership definition.
- [Healthcheck](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/healthcheck/healthcheck) — A Cloudflare standalone Health Check — monitors an origin server from Cloudflare's edge and powers Health Check notifications and analytics.
- [Hold](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/zone/hold) — A Cloudflare zone hold (`/zones/{zone_id}/hold`) — prevents the zone's hostname (and optionally its subdomains) from being added to another Cloudflare account while the hold is active.
- [Hostname](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/web3/hostname) — A Cloudflare Web3 gateway hostname — serve Ethereum or IPFS content from a hostname on your zone via Cloudflare's distributed web gateways.
- [HostnameAssociation](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/certificateauthorities/hostnameassociation) — The set of hostnames in a Cloudflare zone that enforce mTLS, optionally keyed by an uploaded mTLS CA certificate.
- [HostnameAssociation](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/origintlsclientauth/hostnameassociation) — A per-hostname Authenticated Origin Pulls (AOP) association (`/zones/{zone_id}/origin_tls_client_auth/hostnames`).
- [HostnameCertificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/origintlsclientauth/hostnamecertificate) — A per-hostname Authenticated Origin Pulls (AOP) client certificate (`/zones/{zone_id}/origin_tls_client_auth/hostnames/certificates`).
- [HostnameContentList](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/web3/hostnamecontentlist) — The IPFS content-blocking list of a Web3 universal-path gateway hostname — blocks specific CIDs or content paths from being served.
- [HostnameRoute](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/hostnameroute) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **hostname route** — routes traffic for a private hostname through a `cfd_tunnel`, so WARP clients can reach internal apps by name without publishing a public DNS record.
- [HostnameTlsSetting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/hostnametlssetting/hostnametlssetting) — A per-hostname TLS setting override (`/zones/{zone_id}/hostnames/settings/{settingId}/{hostname}`) — pins `ciphers`, `min_tls_version`, or `http2` for a single hostname instead of the whole zone.
- [IdentityProvider](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/identityprovider) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Access identity provider — the login method (one-time PIN, generic OIDC/SAML, or a named provider like GitHub, Google, Okta, or Azure AD) users authenticate with before Access policies evaluate.
- [Images](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/images/images) — A Cloudflare Images binding for image transformation inside Workers — a Worker-only binding with no backing cloud resource.
- [ImpersonationRegistryEntry](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/impersonationregistryentry) — A Cloudflare Email Security (Area 1) impersonation registry entry — maps a protected display name (e.g. a VIP) to their legitimate email address for BEC/impersonation detection.
- [Index](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vectorize/index) — A Cloudflare Vectorize index for storing and querying vector embeddings.
- [IndicatorFeed](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/intel/indicatorfeed) — A Cloudflare custom Indicator Feed (Cloudforce One threat intelligence).
- [IndicatorFeedPermission](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/intel/indicatorfeedpermission) — A permission grant on a Cloudflare custom Indicator Feed, giving another Cloudflare account access to consume the feed.
- [InfrastructureTarget](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/infrastructuretarget) — A Cloudflare Access Infrastructure Target — a server (hostname + IPv4/IPv6 address) protected by Access for Infrastructure (SSH).
- [Integration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/riskscoring/integration) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **risk scoring integration** — a Shared Signals Framework (SSF) push integration that exports user risk-score changes to a third-party tenant (currently Okta) so the IdP can react to risky behavior detected by Zero Trust.
- [IpsecTunnel](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/ipsectunnel) — A Magic Transit / Magic WAN IPsec tunnel between Cloudflare and a customer device.
- [Job](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/logpush/job) — A Cloudflare Logpush job that pushes batches of logs (HTTP requests, Workers trace events, audit logs, Zero Trust datasets, …) to a destination such as R2, S3, GCS, or an HTTP endpoint.
- [KeyConfiguration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/keyconfiguration) — The Cloudflare Zero Trust Access service-key rotation configuration for an account (`/accounts/{account_id}/access/keys`).
- [KeylessCertificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/keylesscertificate/keylesscertificate) — A zone-level Keyless SSL configuration — serve TLS for a certificate whose private key stays on your own key server instead of being uploaded to Cloudflare.
- [Label](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/apishield/label) — A Cloudflare API Shield user label — a zone-scoped tag that can be attached to registered API operations to organize and filter them (e.g. by team, service, or sensitivity).
- [LeakedCredentialCheck](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/leakedcredentialcheck/leakedcredentialcheck) — The Leaked Credential Checks setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/leaked-credential-checks`).
- [LeakedCredentialDetection](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/leakedcredentialcheck/leakedcredentialdetection) — A custom detection location for Cloudflare Leaked Credential Checks (`/zones/{zone_id}/leaked-credential-checks/detections`) — a pair of ruleset expressions telling the WAF where to find the username and password in your application's login requests, so credentials submitted in non-standard payloads can still be checked against breach data.
- [LegacyPipeline](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pipelines/legacypipeline) — A **legacy** Cloudflare Pipeline — the original HTTP-ingest → R2 batch product (`/accounts/{account}/pipelines`).
- [List](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/gateway/list) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Gateway list — a named set of domains, IPs, URLs, emails, serial numbers, or device IDs referenced from Gateway rule wirefilter expressions by UUID (`$<listId>`).
- [List](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/rules/list) — A Cloudflare account-level List (Lists API) — a named collection of IP addresses, ASNs, hostnames, or URL redirects referenced from ruleset expressions (`ip.src in $my_list`) and Bulk Redirect rules.
- [LiveInput](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/stream/liveinput) — A Cloudflare Stream live input — an ingest endpoint (RTMPS/SRT/WebRTC) that accepts live video and optionally records it as a Stream video.
- [LiveInputOutput](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/stream/liveinputoutput) — A Cloudflare Stream live input output — restreams (simulcasts) live video received by a `LiveInput` to another RTMP(S) destination such as YouTube Live or Twitch.
- [LoadBalancer](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/loadbalancer/loadbalancer) — A Cloudflare Load Balancer — a zone-level DNS hostname that distributes traffic across Pools with health-based failover, geo/latency steering, and session affinity.
- [Location](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/gateway/location) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Gateway DNS location — a configured source of DNS traffic (an office, a home network, a device fleet) with its own DNS-over-HTTPS endpoint and optional dedicated destination IPs.
- [Lockdown](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/firewall/lockdown) — A Cloudflare Zone Lockdown rule — restrict one or more URL patterns on a zone so that only an allow-list of IP addresses and CIDR ranges can access them. Every other visitor receives an \"Access Denied\" page.
- [Logging](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/gateway/logging) — Manages the **singleton** Cloudflare Zero Trust **Gateway logging settings** for an account (`/accounts/{accountId}/gateway/logging`) — PII redaction and per-rule-type (DNS / HTTP / L4) activity-log toggles.
- [LogsRetentionFlag](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/logscontrol/logsretentionflag) — The zone-level Logpull retention flag (`/zones/{zone_id}/logs/control/retention/flag`) pinned to a desired value.
- [MagicApp](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicapp) — A custom Magic WAN app — a named set of hostnames and/or IP subnets used for traffic steering and policy decisions, complementing Cloudflare's managed app definitions.
- [MagicSite](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicsite) — A Magic WAN site — represents a physical or logical network location (typically backed by a Magic WAN Connector appliance) under which LANs, WANs, and ACLs are configured.
- [MagicSiteAcl](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicsiteacl) — An ACL between two LANs of a Magic WAN site — allows traffic between LAN segments behind a Magic WAN Connector (all inter-LAN traffic is denied by default).
- [MagicSiteLan](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicsitelan) — A LAN attached to a Magic WAN site — describes a local network segment behind a Magic WAN Connector port (VLAN, addressing, routed subnets, NAT).
- [MagicSiteWan](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicsitewan) — A WAN attached to a Magic WAN site — describes an uplink on a Magic WAN Connector port (addressing, VLAN, load-balancing priority). Cloudflare automatically creates IPsec tunnels over each WAN.
- [MagicStaticRoute](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicstaticroute) — A Magic Transit / Magic WAN static route — steers traffic for a prefix to a next-hop (usually a Magic tunnel interface address).
- [ManagedTransforms](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/managedtransforms/managedtransforms) — The managed request/response header transforms of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/managed_headers`) — a zone-scoped **singleton**: every zone always carries the full catalog of managed transforms (each with an enabled flag), so there is no create or delete on the Cloudflare side.
- [McpPortal](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/mcpportal) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **AI Controls MCP portal** — a hosted gateway that aggregates MCP servers behind a single Access-protected hostname so administrators can govern which AI tools and prompts are exposed to users.
- [Member](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/account/member) — A member of a Cloudflare account — an invitation for a user (by email) to join the account with a set of roles or scoped policies.
- [MetadataIndex](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vectorize/metadataindex) — A metadata index on a Cloudflare Vectorize index.
- [Monitor](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/loadbalancer/monitor) — A Cloudflare Load Balancing monitor — an active health check (HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, ICMP, or SMTP probe) that Load Balancing pools reference to decide which origins are healthy.
- [MonitorGroup](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/loadbalancer/monitorgroup) — A Cloudflare Load Balancing monitor group — aggregates several Monitors into one health signal that a Pool can reference via `monitorGroup` (mutually exclusive with `monitor`).
- [MtlsCertificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/mtlscertificate/mtlscertificate) — An account-level Cloudflare mTLS certificate.
- [Namespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/artifacts/namespace) — A Cloudflare Artifacts namespace — the top-level container for Git-compatible versioned repositories. See the Artifacts launch post and Namespaces docs.
- [Namespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/kv/namespace) — A Cloudflare Workers KV namespace for key-value storage at the edge.
- [NetworkInterconnectSettings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/networkinterconnects/networkinterconnectsettings) — Account-level settings for Cloudflare Network Interconnect (CNI v2) — currently the default ASN applied to new CNI BGP configurations (`/accounts/{account_id}/cni/settings`).
- [NotificationPolicy](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/alerting/notificationpolicy) — A Cloudflare Notifications policy.
- [NotificationWebhook](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/alerting/notificationwebhook) — A Cloudflare Notifications webhook destination.
- [ObservabilityDestination](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/observabilitydestination) — A Workers observability destination — an account-level OTLP export of Workers Logs telemetry (traces, logs, or metrics) pushed to an external HTTPS collector via Logpush.
- [OnRamp](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magiccloudnetworking/onramp) — A Magic Cloud Networking on-ramp — connects cloud VPCs/VNets to Magic WAN by provisioning VPN/Transit-Gateway constructs inside the cloud account registered via a `CloudIntegration`.
- [Operation](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/apishield/operation) — A Cloudflare API Shield operation — a registered API endpoint on a zone, identified by the `(method, host, endpoint)` tuple. Registered operations are the unit other API Shield features (schema validation, rate limiting recommendations, API Discovery) attach to.
- [OperationSetting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/schemavalidation/operationsetting) — A per-operation schema validation override (`/zones/{zone_id}/schema_validation/settings/operations/{operation_id}`) — pins a mitigation action for a single API Shield operation, superseding the zone-level default just for that operation.
- [Organization](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/organization) — Account-level Cloudflare Zero Trust organization settings — the team domain, login branding, session lifetimes, WARP authentication toggle, etc.
- [Organization](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/organization/organization) — A Cloudflare Organization — the hierarchical container above accounts.
- [OriginCaCertificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/origincacertificate/origincacertificate) — A Cloudflare Origin CA certificate — a free certificate signed by Cloudflare's Origin CA that encrypts traffic between Cloudflare's edge and your origin server. Origin CA certificates are only trusted by Cloudflare (not by browsers), so they are used together with proxied DNS records.
- [OriginCloudRegion](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cache/origincloudregion) — An origin cloud-region mapping of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/origin_cloud_regions`).
- [OriginPostQuantumEncryption](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/originpostquantumencryption/originpostquantumencryption) — Origin Post-Quantum Encryption for a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/origin_post_quantum_encryption`).
- [PageRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pagerule/pagerule) — A Cloudflare **Page Rule** — a legacy zone-level rule that matches a URL pattern (`target`) and applies a set of `actions` (cache settings, SSL mode, redirects, etc.) at a given `priority`.
- [Pipeline](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pipelines/pipeline) — A Cloudflare SQL Pipeline — the transform of the Pipelines product. A pipeline is a single SQL statement that reads events from a Stream and writes them to a Sink, both referenced by name.
- [Policy](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/policy) — A reusable, account-scoped Cloudflare Access policy. Distinct from the inline policies attached directly to an `Application` — a reusable policy can be referenced by multiple applications by id.
- [Policy](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pageshield/policy) — A Page Shield policy — a Content Security Policy rule (`/zones/{zone_id}/page_shield/policies`) that is applied when its expression matches a request.
- [Pool](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/loadbalancer/pool) — A Cloudflare Load Balancing pool — an account-scoped group of origin servers that Load Balancers route traffic to. Pools optionally reference a Monitor for active health checking.
- [Prefix](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/addressing/prefix) — A BYOIP (Bring Your Own IP) prefix onboarded to Cloudflare's network.
- [PrefixDelegation](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/addressing/prefixdelegation) — Delegates part of a BYOIP prefix to another Cloudflare account, allowing that account to use the delegated CIDR (e.g. for its own service bindings).
- [Preset](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/realtimekit/preset) — A Cloudflare RealtimeKit preset — a named participant role (e.g. `host`, `guest`) bundling permissions, media quality, and UI design tokens for a RealtimeKit app.
- [Profile](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dlp/profile) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **DLP custom profile** — a named collection of detection entries (regular expressions) that Gateway HTTP policies and CASB integrations reference to detect sensitive data in transit.
- [Project](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pages/project) — A Cloudflare Pages project (direct-upload).
- [ProviderKey](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/providerkey) — Declares a Cloudflare AI Gateway BYOK provider key.
- [ProxyEndpoint](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/gateway/proxyendpoint) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Gateway proxy endpoint — an agentless HTTP proxy for forwarding traffic to Gateway without installing the WARP client, typically wired up via a PAC file pointing at the endpoint's server-assigned `subdomain`.
- [QueryDatabase](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/d1/querydatabase) — Bind a Database to a Worker and obtain the Effect-native D1 connection client (`prepare`, `exec`, `batch`, `raw`).
- [QueryGateway](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/querygateway) — Binding service that turns a Gateway resource into a typed QueryGatewayClient for Worker runtime code. Wraps the Cloudflare.AI. Gateway runtime binding so each operation returns an Effect tagged with GatewayError, exposes the raw Workers AI handle for `ai.run(...)`, and provides a `model(options)` factory that produces an `effect/unstable/ai` `LanguageModel` `Layer`.
- [QuerySearch](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/querysearch) — Bind a SearchInstance to a Worker and obtain the Effect-native AI Search client (`search`, `chatCompletions`, `info`, `stats`). The single-instance `ai_search` binding resolves directly to a runtime `SearchInstance`.
- [QuerySearchNamespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/querysearchnamespace) — Bind a SearchNamespace to a Worker and obtain the Effect-native namespace client whose `.get(name)` selects an instance at runtime. The `ai_search_namespace` binding resolves to a runtime `SearchNamespace` whose `.get(name)` selects an instance within the namespace at runtime.
- [Queue](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/queues/queue) — A Cloudflare Queue for reliable message passing between Workers.
- [RateLimit](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/ratelimit) — A Cloudflare Rate Limit binding for counting arbitrary keys inside Workers — a Worker-only binding with no backing cloud resource.
- [ReadDns](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/readdns) — Binding that lets a Worker read Cloudflare DNS records at runtime.
- [ReadFlags](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/flagship/readflags) — Bind a App to a Worker and obtain the Effect-native flag-evaluation client (`get`, `getBooleanValue`, `getStringValue`, …).
- [ReadNamespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/kv/readnamespace) — Bind a Namespace to a Worker with read access and obtain the Effect-native KV client (`get`, `getWithMetadata`, `list`).
- [ReadSecret](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/secretsstore/readsecret) — Bind a Secret to a Worker and obtain the Effect-native secret client. The client is itself an `Effect` that resolves to the secret's current value.
- [ReadTunnel](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/readtunnel) — Binding that lets a Worker read Cloudflare Tunnels at runtime.
- [ReadWriteDns](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/readwritedns) — Binding that lets a Worker perform the full Cloudflare DNS record CRUD surface at runtime.
- [ReadWriteNamespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/artifacts/readwritenamespace) — Bind a Cloudflare Artifacts namespace (Namespace) to a Worker and obtain the Effect-native ReadWriteNamespaceClient (read + write: create / list / get / delete / import).
- [ReadWriteNamespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/kv/readwritenamespace) — Bind a Namespace to a Worker with read + write access and obtain the Effect-native KV client (`get`, `getWithMetadata`, `list`, `put`, `delete`).
- [ReadWriteTunnel](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/readwritetunnel) — Binding that lets a Worker perform the full Cloudflare Tunnel CRUD surface at runtime.
- [Record](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/record) — A single DNS record on a Cloudflare-managed zone.
- [RegionalHostname](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/regionalhostname/regionalhostname) — A Regional Hostname restricts which Cloudflare data centers decrypt and service HTTPS traffic for a hostname (Data Localization Suite / Regional Services).
- [RegionalTieredCache](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cache/regionaltieredcache) — The Regional Tiered Cache setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/regional_tiered_cache`).
- [Reserve](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cache/reserve) — The Cache Reserve setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/cache_reserve`).
- [ResourceGroup](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/iam/resourcegroup) — A Cloudflare IAM resource group — a named set of account resources (zones, or the whole account) that fine-grained policies attach to.
- [Route](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/route) — A Cloudflare Tunnel Route attaches a private CIDR to a `cfd_tunnel` so that WARP clients (and other Zero Trust egress paths) can reach private IPs through the tunnel.
- [Routing](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/routing) — Enables Cloudflare Email Routing on a zone. This is the prerequisite for receiving mail at any address on the domain and for sending email from a Worker via `send_email` bindings.
- [RpcDurableObject](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/rpcdurableobject) — `RpcDurableObject` is sugar over DurableObject for Durable Objects whose surface is a typed Effect `RpcGroup`. The DO serves an `RpcServer.toHttpEffect(group)` on its own `fetch`, and consumers see `namespace.getByName(id)` as a typed `RpcClient` directly — no manual client wiring.
- [RpcWorker](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/rpcworker) — `RpcWorker` is a thin sugar over Worker for the common case where a worker's entire `fetch` surface is a typed Effect `RpcGroup`. It takes the rpc `schema` directly in props alongside `main`, and accepts an init Effect that returns the already-piped `RpcServer.toHttpEffect(...)`-producing Effect (no `{ fetch }` wrapper) — the wrapper plugs it into the worker's `fetch` for you.
- [Rule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/rule) — A Cloudflare Email Routing rule.
- [Rule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/gateway/rule) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Gateway rule.
- [Rule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magicnetworkmonitoring/rule) — A Magic Network Monitoring (MNM) rule — alerts when traffic to a set of IPv4 prefixes exceeds a static threshold (`threshold`), deviates from the learned baseline (`zscore`), or matches advanced DDoS criteria (`advanced_ddos`, Magic Transit only).
- [Rule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/rum/rule) — A Cloudflare Web Analytics (RUM) rule.
- [Rule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tokenvalidation/rule) — An API Shield JWT validation rule — selects operations/hosts on a zone and enforces a token validation expression with a `log` or `block` action.
- [Rules](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cloudconnector/rules) — The ordered list of Cloud Connector rules for a Cloudflare zone.
- [Ruleset](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ruleset/ruleset) — A Cloudflare Ruleset phase entrypoint for a zone.
- [ScanConfig](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cloudforceone/scanconfig) — A Cloudforce One attack-surface scan configuration.
- [SchemaValidationSchema](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/schemavalidation/schemavalidationschema) — An OpenAPI v3 schema uploaded to a zone for API Shield schema validation (`/zones/{zone_id}/schema_validation/schemas`).
- [Search](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/search) — A convenience construct over SearchInstance that auto-creates the sub-resources an AI Search instance typically needs, so a single call wires up a working pipeline. The data source is chosen by what you pass as `source` — an Bucket for R2, or a URL for a web crawl:
- [SearchIndex](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vectorize/searchindex) — Bind a Index to a Worker and obtain the Effect-native Vectorize client (`describe`, `query`, `upsert`, …).
- [SearchInstance](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/searchinstance) — A Cloudflare.AI. Search (formerly AutoRAG) instance — a fully managed retrieval-augmented generation pipeline over your own data.
- [SearchNamespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/searchnamespace) — A Cloudflare.AI. Search namespace — a logical grouping for AI Search instances within an account.
- [SearchToken](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/searchtoken) — A Cloudflare.AI. Search service token — the credential AI Search uses to access your data source (R2 bucket, Vectorize index, Workers AI) when indexing.
- [Secret](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/secretsstore/secret) — A single secret stored inside a Cloudflare Secrets Store.
- [SecuritySettings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ai/securitysettings) — AI Security for Apps (Firewall for AI) settings on a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/ai-security/settings`).
- [SecurityTxt](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/securitytxt/securitytxt) — A zone's `security.txt` file (`/zones/{zone_id}/security-center/securitytxt`), served by the Cloudflare edge at `https://<zone>/.well-known/security.txt` per RFC 9116 so security researchers know how to report vulnerabilities.
- [Send](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/send) — Bind a SendEmail `send_email` descriptor to a Worker and obtain the Effect-native client for sending email.
- [SendingSubdomain](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/sendingsubdomain) — Registers a Cloudflare Email Sending subdomain on a zone, enabling the account to send transactional email from addresses on that subdomain.
- [ServiceBinding](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/addressing/servicebinding) — Binds part of a BYOIP prefix to a Cloudflare service (CDN, Spectrum, or Magic Transit), routing traffic for the bound CIDR to that service.
- [ServiceToken](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/servicetoken) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Access service token. Service tokens let machine-to-machine clients authenticate to Access-protected applications by sending the `CF-Access-Client-ID` / `CF-Access-Client-Secret` headers.
- [Setting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/origintlsclientauth/setting) — The zone-level Authenticated Origin Pulls (AOP) toggle (`/zones/{zone_id}/origin_tls_client_auth/settings`).
- [Setting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/zone/setting) — A single Cloudflare zone setting (`/zones/{zone_id}/settings/{settingId}`) pinned to a desired value.
- [Settings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pageshield/settings) — The Page Shield configuration of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/page_shield`).
- [Settings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/schemavalidation/settings) — Zone-level schema validation settings (`/zones/{zone_id}/schema_validation/settings`) — the default mitigation action applied to requests that do not conform to an enabled schema, plus an optional zone-wide kill switch.
- [Settings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/waitingroom/settings) — Zone-wide Cloudflare Waiting Room settings (`/zones/{zone_id}/waiting_rooms/settings`).
- [Share](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/resourcesharing/share) — A Cloudflare resource share — shares account-level configuration (gateway policies, custom rulesets, IdP federation grants, …) with another account or organization.
- [ShareRecipient](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/resourcesharing/sharerecipient) — A recipient on an existing Cloudflare share — grants another account or organization access to the share's resources.
- [ShareResource](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/resourcesharing/shareresource) — A resource entry on an existing Cloudflare share — adds a shareable resource (gateway policy, custom ruleset, …) to a `Share` incrementally.
- [SigningKey](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/images/signingkey) — A Cloudflare Images signing key — an HMAC key used to generate signed image delivery URLs (`?sig=` tokens) for images that require signed URLs.
- [SigningKey](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/stream/signingkey) — A Cloudflare Stream signing key — an RSA key pair used to sign viewer playback tokens for videos that require signed URLs.
- [Silence](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/alerting/silence) — A Cloudflare Notifications silence window.
- [Sink](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pipelines/sink) — A Cloudflare Pipelines sink — the destination of the Pipelines product. A SQL Pipeline reads events from a Stream and writes them to a sink, which stores them in R2 either as raw files (`r2`) or as Iceberg tables via the R2 Data Catalog (`r2_data_catalog`).
- [Site](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/rum/site) — A Cloudflare Web Analytics (RUM) site.
- [SmartRouting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/argo/smartrouting) — Argo Smart Routing for a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/argo/smart_routing`).
- [SmartTieredCache](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cache/smarttieredcache) — The Smart Tiered Cache setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/tiered_cache_smart_topology_enable`).
- [Snippet](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/snippets/snippet) — A Cloudflare Snippet — a lightweight JavaScript module that runs on Cloudflare's edge to modify HTTP traffic for a zone.
- [SnippetRules](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/snippets/snippetrules) — The ordered list of snippet rules for a Cloudflare zone.
- [StaticSite](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/website/staticsite) — A Cloudflare Worker that serves static assets built by a shell command.
- [Store](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/secretsstore/store) — A Cloudflare Secrets Store, a per-account container for secrets that can be bound into Workers with full redaction and audit support.
- [Stream](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pipelines/stream) — A Cloudflare Pipelines stream — the ingestion endpoint of the Pipelines product. Events are sent to a stream over HTTP (and/or from Workers via a binding), transformed by a SQL Pipeline, and written to a Sink.
- [Subdomain](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/subdomain) — The account-wide `workers.dev` subdomain singleton (`/accounts/{account_id}/workers/subdomain`). Every Worker with `workers.dev` enabled is served at `https://<script>.<subdomain>.workers.dev`.
- [Subscription](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/queues/subscription) — A Cloudflare Queues event subscription — delivers platform events (R2 bucket events, KV namespace events, Workers Builds, Workflows, etc.) into a Queue as messages.
- [SynProtectionFilter](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ddosprotection/synprotectionfilter) — An Advanced TCP Protection SYN Protection filter (Magic Transit).
- [SynProtectionRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ddosprotection/synprotectionrule) — An Advanced TCP Protection SYN flood rule (Magic Transit).
- [Tag](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/access/tag) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Access tag. Tags are plain labels that can be attached to Access applications to group and filter them.
- [TcpFlowProtectionFilter](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ddosprotection/tcpflowprotectionfilter) — An Advanced TCP Protection TCP Flow Protection filter (Magic Transit).
- [TcpFlowProtectionRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ddosprotection/tcpflowprotectionrule) — An Advanced TCP Protection out-of-state TCP flow rule (Magic Transit).
- [TestSchedule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/speed/testschedule) — A recurring Cloudflare Observatory (Speed) test schedule — Cloudflare runs a Lighthouse test against a page on your zone on a `DAILY` or `WEEKLY` cadence from a region of your choice.
- [TieredCaching](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/argo/tieredcaching) — Tiered Caching for a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/argo/tiered_caching`).
- [TokenConfiguration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tokenvalidation/tokenconfiguration) — An API Shield JWT validation token configuration — the JWKS key material and token source locations used to validate JSON Web Tokens on a zone.
- [TotalTls](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/acm/totaltls) — The Total TLS setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/acm/total_tls`).
- [TrustedDomain](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/trusteddomain) — A Cloudflare Email Security (Area 1) trusted domain — exempts a domain from recently-registered and lookalike (similarity) detections.
- [Tunnel](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/tunnel) — A Cloudflare Tunnel that establishes a secure connection from your origin to Cloudflare's edge.
- [TurnKey](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/calls/turnkey) — A Cloudflare Realtime (formerly \"Calls\") TURN key.
- [UaRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/firewall/uarule) — A Cloudflare User Agent Blocking rule — block or challenge every request to a zone whose `User-Agent` header exactly matches a given string.
- [UniversalSsl](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ssl/universalssl) — The Universal SSL setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/ssl/universal/settings`).
- [UrlNormalization](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/urlnormalization/urlnormalization) — The URL normalization configuration of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/url_normalization`) — a zone-scoped **singleton** controlling how Cloudflare normalizes incoming URLs before rule matching and before forwarding to the origin.
- [UserApiToken](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/apitoken/userapitoken) — A Cloudflare user-owned API token (`POST /user/tokens`).
- [UserGroup](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/iam/usergroup) — A Cloudflare IAM user group — a named set of account members that share fine-grained policies (permission groups scoped to resource groups).
- [UserGroupMembership](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/iam/usergroupmembership) — Membership of a single account member in a Cloudflare IAM user group.
- [UserSchema](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/apishield/userschema) — A Cloudflare API Shield user schema — an OpenAPI v3 document uploaded to a zone for (legacy \"classic\") schema validation of API traffic.
- [Variant](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/images/variant) — A Cloudflare Images variant — a named resizing preset (e.g. `thumbnail`, `hero`) applied when serving images from Cloudflare Images.
- [Variants](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cache/variants) — The Variants cache setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/variants`).
- [VersionMetadata](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/versionmetadata) — A Cloudflare Workers Version Metadata binding — a Worker-only binding with no backing cloud resource. Cloudflare provides the deployed Worker version at runtime (`id`, `tag`, `timestamp`).
- [View](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/view) — An Internal DNS view (`/accounts/{account_id}/dns_settings/views`) — a named set of internal zones that DNS queries can be resolved against, for split-horizon / internal DNS setups.
- [VirtualNetwork](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/virtualnetwork) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Virtual Network — an isolated routing namespace for Cloudflare Tunnel private networks.
- [Vite](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/website/vite) — A Cloudflare Worker deployed from a Vite project.
- [VpcService](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vpcservice/vpcservice) — A Cloudflare VPC service that exposes a private host (IP or hostname) reachable through a Cloudflare Tunnel for Workers VPC.
- [VpcServiceRef](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vpcservice/vpcserviceref) — Reference an existing Cloudflare VPC service without managing its lifecycle.
- [VulnScannerCredential](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vulnerabilityscanner/vulnscannercredential) — A credential inside a Cloudflare Vulnerability Scanner credential set — an HTTP header or cookie value the DAST scanner attaches to outgoing requests so it can scan authenticated surfaces.
- [VulnScannerCredentialSet](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vulnerabilityscanner/vulnscannercredentialset) — A Cloudflare Vulnerability Scanner credential set — a named container for authentication credentials (headers/cookies) the DAST scanner attaches to outgoing requests when scanning authenticated surfaces.
- [VulnScannerTargetEnvironment](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vulnerabilityscanner/vulnscannertargetenvironment) — A Cloudflare Vulnerability Scanner target environment — declares which zone the DAST-style web vulnerability scanner (Security Center, beta) is allowed to scan.
- [WaitingRoom](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/waitingroom/waitingroom) — A Cloudflare Waiting Room — places visitors in a virtual queue when traffic to a host + path exceeds the configured thresholds, protecting the origin from overload.
- [WarpConnector](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/warpconnector) — A Cloudflare WARP Connector tunnel — a software site-to-site connector that extends a private network into Cloudflare Zero Trust without running `cloudflared`.
- [Watermark](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/stream/watermark) — A Cloudflare Stream watermark profile — a PNG image stamped onto videos at upload time.
- [Webhook](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/realtimekit/webhook) — A Cloudflare RealtimeKit webhook — receives meeting, recording, livestream, transcript, and summary events for a RealtimeKit app.
- [Webhook](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/stream/webhook) — The Cloudflare Stream webhook — an **account-level singleton** that receives notifications when videos finish processing or live inputs connect/disconnect.
- [Widget](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/turnstile/widget) — A Cloudflare Turnstile widget — Cloudflare's CAPTCHA alternative.
- [Worker](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/worker) — A Cloudflare Worker host with deploy-time binding support and runtime export collection.
- [WorkerLoader](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/workerloader) — Load and run ephemeral Workers at runtime from inline JavaScript modules.
- [WorkerRoute](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/workerroute) — A Workers Route — a zone-level mapping from a URL pattern to a Worker script.
- [Workflow](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workflows/workflow) — A Cloudflare Workflow that orchestrates durable, multi-step tasks with automatic retries and at-least-once delivery.
- [WriteDataset](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/analyticsengine/writedataset) — Bind an AnalyticsEngineDatasetLike dataset to a Worker and obtain the Effect-native client (`writeDataPoint`, `raw`).
- [WriteDns](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/writedns) — Binding that lets a Worker create, update, and delete Cloudflare DNS records at runtime.
- [WriteNamespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/kv/writenamespace) — Bind a Namespace to a Worker with write access and obtain the Effect-native KV client (`put`, `delete`).
- [WriteQueue](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/queues/writequeue) — Binding service that turns a Queue resource into a typed WriteQueueClient you can call from a Worker's runtime Effect.
- [WriteTunnel](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/writetunnel) — Binding that lets a Worker create, update, and delete Cloudflare Tunnels at runtime.
- [Zone](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/zone/zone) — A Cloudflare Zone (DNS domain) managed by Alchemy.
- [ZoneDnsSettings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonednssettings) — The DNS settings of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/dns_settings`) — nameserver assignment, NS TTL, SOA components, CNAME flattening, multi-provider mode, and zone mode.
- [ZoneResourceTags](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tags/zoneresourcetags) — Key/value tags attached to a zone-level Cloudflare resource via the unified resource-tagging API (open beta).
- [ZoneTransferAcl](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonetransferacl) — A Secondary DNS zone-transfer ACL (`/accounts/{account_id}/secondary_dns/acls`) — an account-wide IPv4/IPv6 range that may receive NOTIFYs for secondary zones and from which Cloudflare accepts AXFR/IXFR requests for outgoing transfers.
- [ZoneTransferIncoming](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonetransferincoming) — The incoming zone-transfer configuration of a secondary zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/secondary_dns/incoming`) — links the zone to the peers Cloudflare transfers it in from and sets the auto-refresh interval.
- [ZoneTransferOutgoing](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonetransferoutgoing) — The outgoing zone-transfer configuration of a primary zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/secondary_dns/outgoing`) — links the zone to the peers Cloudflare NOTIFYs and serves AXFR/IXFR to, and toggles transfers on or off via the dedicated enable/disable endpoints.
- [ZoneTransferPeer](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonetransferpeer) — A Secondary DNS zone-transfer peer (`/accounts/{account_id}/secondary_dns/peers`) — an external nameserver Cloudflare exchanges zone transfers with. Link peers to a zone via ZoneTransferIncoming (secondary zones) or ZoneTransferOutgoing (primary zones).
- [ZoneTransferTsig](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonetransfertsig) — A Secondary DNS TSIG key (`/accounts/{account_id}/secondary_dns/tsigs`) — shared-secret authentication for zone transfers between Cloudflare and external nameservers. Reference it from a ZoneTransferPeer via `tsigId`.

### Command

- [Build](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/command/build) — A `Build` runs a shell command that produces an output asset (a file or directory) and tracks that asset in state. Unlike `Exec`, a `Build` has an output contract: `reconcile` verifies the command actually produced `outdir` and exposes its location so downstream resources (e.g. a `Cloudflare.Worker`'s static assets) can consume it.
- [Dev](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/command/dev) — A long-lived shell process scoped to a stack instance, started during `alchemy dev` and restarted when its inputs change. During `alchemy deploy` this is a no-op — `Dev` resources only run in dev mode.
- [Exec](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/command/exec) — An `Exec` runs a shell command purely for its side effects — it has no output contract. Unlike `Build`, it does not produce or track an output asset; `reconcile` runs the command and the resource succeeds as long as the command exits with code `0` (a non-zero exit fails with a `CommandError`).

### Docker

- [Container](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/docker/container) — A Docker container managed through the active Docker context.
- [Image](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/docker/image) — Builds, tags, and optionally pushes Docker images through the active Docker context.
- [Network](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/docker/network) — A Docker network managed through the active Docker context.
- [RemoteImage](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/docker/remoteimage) — Pulls a remote Docker image through the active Docker context, optionally re-tagging it and pushing it to a registry.
- [Volume](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/docker/volume) — A Docker volume managed through the active Docker context.

### Drizzle

- [postgres](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/drizzle/postgres) — Open a Drizzle/Postgres database from a connection URL using the `drizzle-orm/effect-postgres` integration.
- [Schema](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/drizzle/schema) — A Drizzle schema managed as an Alchemy resource.

### GitHub

- [Comment](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/comment) — A GitHub issue or pull request comment.
- [Repository](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/repository) — A GitHub repository.
- [Secret](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/secret) — A GitHub Actions repository or environment secret.
- [Secrets](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/secrets) — Bulk-creates a set of Secrets in the same repository (and optionally the same environment).
- [Variable](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/variable) — A GitHub Actions repository variable.
- [Variables](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/variables) — Bulk-creates a set of Variables in the same repository.
- [Webhook](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/webhook) — A GitHub repository webhook.

### Kubernetes

- [ConfigMap](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/configmap) — A Kubernetes config map bound to an EKS cluster.
- [Deployment](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/deployment) — A Kubernetes deployment bound to an EKS cluster.
- [Job](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/job) — A Kubernetes job bound to an EKS cluster.
- [Namespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/namespace) — A Kubernetes namespace bound to an EKS cluster.
- [Service](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/service) — A Kubernetes service bound to an EKS cluster.
- [ServiceAccount](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/serviceaccount) — A Kubernetes service account bound to an EKS cluster.

### Neon

- [Branch](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/neon/branch) — A branch of a Neon project.
- [Project](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/neon/project) — A Neon serverless Postgres project.

### Planetscale

- [MySQLBranch](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/mysql/mysqlbranch) — A PlanetScale branch of a MySQLDatabase. For PostgreSQL branches use PostgresBranch instead.
- [MySQLDatabase](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/mysql/mysqldatabase) — A MySQL PlanetScale database (powered by Vitess). For PostgreSQL use PostgresDatabase instead.
- [MySQLPassword](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/mysql/mysqlpassword) — A PlanetScale password for accessing a MySQL database branch.
- [PostgresBranch](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/postgres/postgresbranch) — A PlanetScale branch of a PostgresDatabase. For MySQL branches use MySQLBranch instead.
- [PostgresDatabase](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/postgres/postgresdatabase) — A PostgreSQL PlanetScale database. For MySQL, use MySQLDatabase instead.
- [PostgresDefaultRole](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/postgres/postgresdefaultrole) — The default PlanetScale PostgreSQL role for a database branch.
- [PostgresRole](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/postgres/postgresrole) — A PlanetScale role for accessing a PostgreSQL database branch.
